Why Raul Sanllehi is so important to Sven Mislintat and Arsenal's plans for after Wenger

With Wenger seemingly reluctant to have a director of football installed over his head, Arsenal's new talent-spotting mastermind could serve as a go-between of sorts, hired as more than just a chief scout with the jurisdiction to revamp the club's approach to recruitment.

It's worth remembering that back when the club did hold an advantage over their rivals when it came to scouting and recruitment they too boasted a track record of success to put most other teams to shame.

With Wenger's contacts across the continent and knowledge of the French market in particular, and the negotiating skills and counsel of David Dein, in came the likes of Emmanuel Petit, Freddie Ljungberg, Kolo Toure, Marc Overmars, Nicolas Anelka, Patrick Vieira, Robert Pires and Thierry Henry, and the trophies.

If Sanllehi can provide the bargaining brawn to Milslintat's brains, the return of those long lost days may not be so distant.

http://www.football.london/arsenal-fc/transfer-news/mislintat-sanllehi-arsenal-wenger-news-13927542

What does it mean for Arsene Wenger?

The changes to Arsenal’s backroom team point to new visions for the club - which could put Arsene Wenger’s position in to some doubt.

Wenger signed up for two more years in the summer but has admitted his position will be reviewed in the summer.

The Arsenal statement claims Sanllehi will work alongside Wenger, but it points to a shift away from the Frenchman's dictatorship of football business.

https://www.express.co.uk/sport/football/885333/Arsenal-Raul-Sanllehi-Barcelona-Arsene-Wenger

Arsenal player ratings: Nelson the bright spot as Mertesacker, Holding & Iwobi dreadful in Forest loss

By Charles WattsArsenal Correspondent

David Ospina

Made some excellent saves in the first half as Forest pressed forward, but possibly could have done better with the first goal as it was swung in to the near post. 6


Rob Holding

Looked sluggish and nervous throughout alongside Mertesacker. Struggled to deal with the pace and intensity of Forest's attacking play. Gave away the penalty to cap an utterly miserable display. 3

Per Mertesacker

The big German scored Arsenal's goal but was given a torrid afternoon by Ben Brereton, he just could cope with the Forest frontman who ran at him every time he could. 3

Mathieu Debuchy

Got forward well on a few occasions, but like his fellow defenders he was pulled put of position far too easily as Forest attacked with pace. 4

Reiss Nelson

A real bright spot in an otherwise miserable 90 minutes. Attacked with intent and looked as likely as anyone to make something happen. 6

Ainsley Maitland-Niles

Combined well with Nelson at times and produced one magical moment of skill in the first half. One of Arsenal's better players. 5

Joe Willock

Never really got into the game at all. Overrun in midfield before being replaced by Nketiah. 4

Mohamed Elneny

No influence at all, despite being one of the most experienced players on the pitch. Offered no protection to the defence and nothing going forward. 4

Alex Iwobi

Another poor performance from a player horribly out of form. Played in a deeper role than normal but couldn't influence the game. 3

Danny Welbeck

Never really looked like scoring, but to his credit he stayed switched on to take advantage of Jordan Smith's mistake. Aside from that a really poor display. 4

Theo Walcott

No impact whatsoever, apart from the free-kick which led to the first goal. Did his chances no good of forcing his way into the Premier League side. 4

Substitutes

Eddie Nketiah

Created one good chance for himself but was denied by Jordan Smith's legs. 5

Chuba Akpom

Thrown on for the last few minutes and missed a good chance to make it 4-3. 5

According to the Mirror, Arsenal could sell Francis Coquelin before the January transfer window closes.

The 26-year-old was a surprise omission from the squad that lost to Nottingham Forest on in the FA Cup on Sunday. It’s understood the player was not injured.

Arsene Wenger spoke last week about the midfielder’s situation at the Emirates claiming he’s suffered as a result of the Gunners using a four-man defence less regularly.

“Life has been a bit unfair with him, it’s true,” said Wenger. “That’s also down a bit to our change in system – he paid a heavy price for that – but overall he’s in a very good shape.

“We’ll have games, many games now, so that will help him keep his focus and maintain his quality at a very high level.”

Coquelin has made only one start in the Premier League this season and that was as a defender in the defeat at Manchester City.

The Frenchman has three and a half years left on his current deal and it’s suggested we could hold out for a fee of £10 million. West Ham United are understood to be his most likely destination.

艾沙雲老婆疑因臭尿片被趕落機

【now.com體育】前阿仙奴球星艾沙雲的太太Alisa最近被空中服務員趕落飛機，有指原因是她為BB女換尿片時的氣味太臭。

報道指，前俄羅斯國家隊隊長艾沙雲（Andrey Arshavin）第二任太太Alisa早前帶同兩名女兒，登上由莫斯科前往哈薩克城市阿拉木圖的航班。起飛之前，Alisa差不多替年幼的BB女Eseniya換完尿片時，疑因發出的臭味被請下飛機，最終她被迫帶著兩女落機。

不過，根據航空公司及當時機上其他人的說法，驅逐事件卻跟臭尿片無關，而是Alisa作出了影響航機安全的行為。

據悉，另一個版本為Alisa當時向空中服務員臨時要求加錢，並讓同行持著經濟艙機票的保母坐進其身處的商務艙，但不獲空服員允許。35歲的Alisa為此不停吵鬧，同時容許年長的女兒在機上亂走，最終才被趕下機，事件更令航班延遲了2小時起飛。
